title: Tender Spring Irish Potato Gratin
description: A savory Irish dish featuring parma ham and salt-baked potatoes, perfect for a crowd, and free from onions and garlic. This recipe is made in individual mini cocottes for a delightful presentation.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot of salted water, boil the sliced potatoes until they are slightly tender.
Drain the potatoes and set them aside to cool.
In a small saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the diced Parma ham to the saucepan and cook until crispy.
In a separate bowl, mix together the heavy cream, grated cheddar cheese, chopped thyme, salt, and black pepper.
In individual mini cocottes, create a layer of potatoes, followed by a spoonful of the cream mixture, and then a sprinkle of crispy Parma ham.
Repeat the layering process until all ingredients are used, finishing with a layer of cream on top.
Bake the gratins in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are tender.
